evals.name: decompose-and-run-via-from-stdin
evals.prompt: I have a one-off task: migrate a service from one database to another. It has clear phases (snapshot the old DB, run the migration, verify row counts match, cut traffic over) and I want koto to govern the order and keep an audit trail. There's no template for this. The koto binary is on PATH. How do I get koto to run this?
evals.expected_output: Agent decomposes the task into ordered states and runs it inline via `koto init <name> --from-stdin`, piping a workflow definition on stdin. It does NOT tell the user to write and save a template file first, and it does NOT redirect to koto-author (this is a one-off with no matching template).
assertions: Response uses `koto init` with `--from-stdin` to run the inline definition (the ephemeral path), not `--template` pointing at a saved file
Response pipes a workflow definition on stdin rather than telling the user to author and commit a durable template file first
Response decomposes the task into multiple ordered states (does not put the whole task in one monolithic state)
Response explains that after init the agent drives the workflow with the `koto next` run loop (per koto-user)
Response does NOT redirect this one-off task to koto-author

evals.name: gate-at-verification-boundary
evals.prompt: I'm decomposing a one-off dependency-upgrade task into a koto workflow to run with `koto init --from-stdin`. The workflow must not record the change in the changelog until the test suite actually passes against the new version. How should I express that the suite must pass before proceeding?
evals.expected_output: Agent puts a gate at the real verification boundary (the test suite passing), routed with a gates.<name>.<field> when clause so the engine resolves it. It distinguishes a gate (objective, machine-checked) from agent-submitted evidence, and notes the strict --from-stdin path requires gates.* routing.
assertions: Response uses a gate (e.g., a `command` gate) at the verification boundary rather than an agent-asserted evidence field for the objective 'tests pass' check
Response routes the gate with a `gates.<name>.<field>` when clause (e.g., `gates.suite.exit_code: 0`) so the engine resolves the transition
Response notes that the `--from-stdin` path is strict and a gate without `gates.*` routing is rejected
Response does NOT suggest using `--allow-legacy-gates` on the `--from-stdin` path

evals.name: repeated-authoring-redirect-to-koto-author
evals.prompt: Over the past week I've used `koto init --from-stdin` three times to run essentially the same release-checklist workflow, re-typing almost the same definition each time. It works, but it's getting repetitive. Should I keep doing this?
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes this is no longer a one-off and steers the user to koto-author to build a durable, committed template, rather than continuing to re-pipe near-identical definitions. It explains that --from-stdin is for ephemeral one-offs and a recurring workflow shape belongs in a durable template.
assertions: Response recommends switching to koto-author to author a durable template for this recurring workflow
Response explains that `--from-stdin` is for one-off tasks and should not be a standing substitute for a durable template
Response does NOT recommend continuing to re-pipe the same definition via `--from-stdin` as the durable solution

evals.name: negative-existing-template-redirect
evals.prompt: I already have a koto template for our code-review workflow at `.koto/review.md`. I need to start a session and run it on PR 512. How do I do that?
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes a template already exists, so this is a plain workflow-execution task: run it with `koto init --template` and the koto-user run loop. It does NOT author an inline --from-stdin definition for a task that already has a template.
assertions: Response uses `koto init` with `--template .koto/review.md` (the existing template), not `--from-stdin`
Response does NOT author or pipe an inline workflow definition for a task that already has a matching template
Response refers to the koto-user run loop (`koto next`) for executing the workflow
